contracts.label: Places review window
contracts.description: Preferred dynamic slice for viewport-bounded place geometry once binary publication is enabled.
contracts.cache_strategy: Cache by bbox and zoom bucket in the client for five minutes and publish immutable FlatGeobuf objects once the package build lands.

reasons: Neighborhood and ward review need geometry fidelity without shipping whole-city GeoJSON to phones.
FlatGeobuf keeps the viewport contract range-request friendly before full scene packets are assembled.

notes: The JSON fallback remains valid for baseline and low-bandwidth modes.
This contract becomes the preferred input to scene packet assembly for map-facing place layers.

contracts.label: Street safety corridor slices
contracts.description: Corridor-level safety review uses FlatGeobuf slices keyed to the current viewport and zoom bucket.
contracts.cache_strategy: Cache corridor slices per bbox, zoom bucket, and event recency window; prefer immutable object URLs with HTTP range support when published.

reasons: Street-level safety review needs medium-sized dynamic subsets that are too heavy for eager GeoJSON on phones.
The packet compiler can reuse the same viewport slice without re-scanning full event payloads.

notes: This is the primary dynamic review-oriented vector lane for the first mobile packet work.
The selection rule hands off to scene packets once the client requests denser overlays.

contracts.label: Intervention review slices
contracts.description: Intervention and public-commitment review windows publish bounded feature slices instead of full-city fallback payloads.
contracts.cache_strategy: Cache by bbox, zoom bucket, and review state so community review flows stay warm without making Rusty Red canonical.

reasons: Intervention review favors smaller, shape-accurate subsets that are still too large for always-on JSON.
Review-only slices should stay renderer-agnostic so the same packet inputs can map to future native clients.

notes: This rule keeps review-oriented overlays on the same binary lane as map-facing packets.
Fallback remains civic-objects JSON until the publication build emits the FlatGeobuf artifact.
